content.problemStatement:
Chef owns several pairs of shoes, unfortunately he has so many that he lost track of them!

Each pair of shoes consists of one left shoe and one right shoe.

Looking at his collection, Chef counts LLL left shoes and RRR right shoes.

What's the minimum number of shoes Chef is missing?


Input Format

The only line of input will contain two space-separated integers LLL and RRR – the number of left shoes and right shoes, respectively.


Output Format
Output a single integer: the minimum number of shoes Chef is missing.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There's one left shoe and three right shoes.
The single left shoe can be paired with one right shoe, leaving two unpaired right shoes. So, the answer is 222.

Explanation:
There are an equal number of left and right shoes, so it's possible to pair them all up.

Explanation:
There are 888 left shoes and 333 right shoes.
Each right shoe can be paired up with one left shoe, which leaves 555 left shoes unpaired.
